question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /nfts endpoint for a victim account with no filters, causing extractNftMultiUnionQuery() to return all NFTs owned by that account along with their spender approvals, revealing the complete NFT authorization state (which spenders can transfer which NFTs) without any ownership verification?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the /allowances/crypto endpoint returns the spender field in the CryptoAllowanceViewModel response, to build a complete map of all entities authorized to spend on behalf of any account on the network, enabling large-scale reconnaissance of authorization relationships without any owner approval or notification?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id eq filter to extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to check whether a specific token allowance exists for a victim account, effectively probing the authorization state of the account for a specific token without the owner's knowledge, and use this to time an attack exploiting that allowance?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/crypto endpoint for the staking reward account (shard.realm.800) or fee collection account (shard.realm.802) to enumerate all entities authorized to spend on behalf of system accounts, revealing privileged authorization relationships that could be exploited to authorize transactions without system account owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple spender.id eq filters to the /allowances/crypto endpoint, causing all values to be aggregated into the spenderInValues array and emitted as a single IN clause, and use this to confirm in a single request whether a set of specific spenders have been authorized by a victim account, without any rate limiting or authentication?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination link generated by getPaginationLink() in the /allowances/crypto response, which encodes the last spender ID as the cursor, to reconstruct the internal spender ID ordering and enumerate all authorized spenders for a victim account across multiple requests without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the multi-union query structure in extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() (lower, inner, upper parts) by supplying boundary spender.id and token.id values that cause the inner query to return records from a broader range than intended, exposing token allowances that were not meant to be visible to unauthorized callers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id eq filter combined with a serial.number range to the /nfts endpoint, causing extractNftMultiUnionQuery() to return specific NFTs along with their spender approvals, confirming whether a specific NFT has been approved for transfer to a specific spender without the owner's knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/crypto endpoint for a victim account using the account's public alias (base32-encoded) as the idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter, and if EntityService.getEncodedId() resolves the alias to the correct account ID, retrieve all crypto allowances without any authorization check, exposing the account's full spending authorization state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/tokens endpoint for a victim account using a long-zero EVM address (0x000...{num}) as the path parameter, causing EntityService.getEncodedId() to resolve it to the account's numeric ID, and retrieve all token allowances without any ownership verification, exposing delegated token transfer authority?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any middleware between the router.getExt() call and the CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances handler to bypass any hypothetical upstream rate limiting by sending requests directly to the /allowances/crypto endpoint, enabling unlimited enumeration of authorized spenders for any account without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id ne filter to the /nfts endpoint, causing validateFilters() to throw an InvalidArgumentError, and use the error response timing or message to infer information about the internal validation logic that could be used to craft more targeted allowance enumeration attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/crypto endpoint with a limit=1 parameter and iterate through all pages using the next pagination link, performing a complete ordered traversal of all authorized spenders for a victim account one at a time, with each request revealing the next spender in the authorization list without any owner approval or notification?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id eq filter matching their own account ID to the /allowances/tokens endpoint for a victim account, confirming whether they themselves have been granted token spending authority on that account, and use this as a real-time check before executing unauthorized token transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the amountCondition (`${CryptoAllowance.AMOUNT} > 0`) is appended after the spenderInValues IN clause in extractCryptoAllowancesQuery(), and if the SQL parameter positions are miscalculated due to the dynamic conditions array, cause the amount filter to be skipped, returning zero-amount (revoked) allowances that appear to still be active?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /nfts endpoint for a victim account with a spender.id eq filter set to the zero account (0.0.0), and if NftService.getNfts() does not properly handle the zero spender case, retrieve NFTs that have no spender approval set, potentially revealing NFTs that are implicitly transferable without explicit owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/crypto endpoint for a victim account and observe the response structure (spender, amount fields in CryptoAllowanceViewModel) to determine the exact amount of HBAR each authorized spender can transfer, enabling the attacker to identify high-value allowances and time attacks to drain those allowances without the owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/tokens endpoint for a victim account and observe the TokenAllowanceViewModel response to determine which tokens and amounts each spender is authorized to transfer, enabling targeted exploitation of high-value token allowances without the account owner's approval or knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the router in accountRoute.js uses extendExpress() which wraps handlers to auto-call next() on promise resolution, and if an error in EntityService.getEncodedId() is swallowed by the wrap() function's catch block, cause the handler to proceed with a null or default accountId, returning allowance data for an unintended account without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token.id with a gte operator and no spender.id filter to extractTokenMultiUnionQuery(), causing the secondary bound to be set without a primary bound, and if validateBounds() does not catch this combination, retrieve token allowances for all spenders for tokens above a threshold without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a serial.number range filter without a token.id filter to the /nfts endpoint, and if validateSecondaryBound() in BaseController does not properly reject this combination (secondary without primary), retrieve NFT allowance data across all tokens for a victim account without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/crypto endpoint for a victim account using an encoded entity ID (large integer) as the path parameter, bypassing any alias-based access controls, and retrieve the full list of authorized spenders without any ownership verification, enabling unauthorized knowledge of the account's transaction authorization state?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the multi-union query structure in extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() by supplying a spender.id eq filter combined with a token.id range, causing the lower, inner, and upper query parts to all include the spender eq condition, and retrieve a broader set of token allowances than intended for that spender on a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/crypto endpoint for a victim account and use the response to identify spenders with large allowances, then impersonate those spenders in off-chain communications or social engineering attacks to authorize transactions without the account owner's direct approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /rewards endpoint for a victim account and retrieve the full staking reward transfer history, revealing the account's staking delegation relationships (staked_node_id, staked_account_id from processRow()), which could be used to infer authorization patterns and craft transactions that exploit those delegation relationships without owner approval?
